questions: Should the 'Switch to Final Video' action be a single undo step, or is it acceptable as two separate history entries (delete clips + insert video)?
Should the dismissed state persist across sessions (e.g., stored in localStorage or the timeline config), or is client-only (resets on remount) sufficient?

When the final video duration doesn't match the shot group time span, should we: (a) trim the video to fit the exact time range, (b) use the video's natural duration and adjust surrounding clips, or (c) stretch/compress to fit?
Should the badge appear on shot groups with only 1 clip (currently useShotGroups requires minimum 2 clips), or should we also show it for single-clip shots that have final videos?

success_criteria.criterion: A video icon badge (sky-500 or similar distinct color) appears on shot group overlays when that shot has a final video in shot_final_videos
Clicking the badge opens a menu with 'Switch to Final Video' and 'Dismiss' options
'Switch to Final Video' removes all image clips in the shot group and inserts a single video clip spanning the same time range
The replacement video clip is properly registered in the asset registry with correct type and generationId
'Dismiss' hides the badge for that shot (client-side only)
Existing useShotGroups.test.ts tests pass without modification
The switch action is a single undo step (one applyEdit call)
Badge styling is visually distinct from the amber stale-variant badge
New hook file stays under ~80 lines; no file exceeds +50 lines of additions
useFinalVideoAvailable has a unit test covering: shot with final video detected, dismissed shot hidden, shot without final video ignored
Manual smoke test: open editor with project that has shot final videos, badge appears, switch works, dismiss works, undo restores original clips

assumptions: Dismissed state is client-only (resets on remount) — same pattern as useStaleVariants dismissedAssetKeys. No persistence needed unless user requests it.
The final video will be trimmed (from/to) to fit the shot group time span rather than using its natural duration, since the goal is to replace the image sequence in-place.
The switch action should be atomic (single applyEdit call = single undo step) for clean undo behavior.
The badge only appears on shot groups that meet the existing minimum-2-clips threshold in useShotGroups. Single-clip shots won't show the badge.
The final video's generationId from shot_final_videos.id is sufficient for asset registration — no additional variant lookup is needed.
useProjectSelectionContext() is available in the TimelineEditor component tree (confirmed: it's used in useTimelineState.ts which is called from the same tree).
